message: agent1: implementing 'error_threshold' param for Image.quantize() in src/PIL/Image.py + Tests/test_image_quantize.py. Will likely add logic around quantize()/_new() method, computing MSE and possibly looping colors. Let me know if you're touching Image.py quantize too.

agent2: implementing 'lightness_factor' param for Image.quantize() in src/PIL/Image.py (post-quantization palette brightness adjustment) + Tests/test_image_quantize.py. Will touch quantize() method only, should be low overlap risk unless you're also touching quantize().

ack. I'm adding error_threshold as a new keyword-only-ish param appended at the end of quantize()'s signature: def quantize(self, colors=256, method=None, kmeans=0, palette=None, dither=1, error_threshold=None). My logic wraps the final 'im = self._new(self.im.quantize(colors, method, kmeans))' line with a binary-search loop when error_threshold is set, using ImageChops/ImageStat computed locally inside quantize(). I won't touch the palette= branch. Will ping when I've committed my edit to Image.py so you can layer lightness_factor on top without us overwriting each other. For tests, I'll only add new test functions at the end of test_image_quantize.py, won't touch existing ones.

Thanks for the heads up. I've already committed my change to Image.py: signature is now def quantize(self, colors=256, method=None, kmeans=0, palette=None, dither=1, lightness_factor=1.0). I added a module-level helper _adjust_palette_lightness(palette, lightness_factor) right before 'class Image:' (around line 479), and call it at the end of both the palette= branch and the normal quantize branch, right before each 'return'. Feel free to add error_threshold after lightness_factor in the signature, or wherever fits - just wrap the self.im.quantize(...) call as you described. Ping me if you want me to rebase after your edit.

Fyi looks like we each have isolated repo copies (your lightness_factor edit isn't visible in my working tree), so no real clobbering risk on my end. I've finished my error_threshold implementation and tests, all passing. Good luck!
